text.title: langchain-agents-architectures Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules covering iteration caps, error handling, plan validation, output truncation, structured supervisors, and tracing.

rule.statement: Every ReAct agent built via `create_react_agent` MUST set `max_iterations` to a value tied to a real workflow length; defaults can be unbounded in some LangChain versions.
rule.rationale: Open iteration caps drain budget when an agent enters a tool-call loop.

rule.statement: Every tool that can fail MUST be decorated with `@tool(handle_tool_error=True)`; never let a tool exception abort a multi-step run.
rule.rationale: Unhandled tool errors propagate and stop the agent; catching them lets the agent retry with different inputs.

rule.statement: Plan-and-Execute agents MUST validate the plan JSON schema before dispatching any execution steps AND add a re-plan gate after each step that checks whether the remaining plan is still valid.
rule.rationale: A failed step 2 leaves steps 3-N invalid; static plans don't account for intermediate results.

rule.statement: Tool outputs MUST be truncated to ~2K characters before appending to LangGraph state; verbose outputs (search dumps, full files) MUST be offloaded per `filesystem-as-working-memory`.
rule.rationale: A 20-step ReAct run with verbose tool outputs can consume 50K+ tokens of state.

rule.statement: Supervisor agents that route between specialists MUST use `model.with_structured_output(Route)` (Pydantic) — NEVER parse a free-text "next agent" string.
rule.rationale: Free-form routing names workers that do not exist; structured output makes routing a typed dispatch.

rule.statement: Production agents MUST emit LangSmith traces with a `session_id` tag for cost attribution and debugging; traces MAY be disabled only in offline batch jobs.
rule.rationale: Without per-step traces, debugging tool-use failures or plan staleness is impossible.
